During verification of previous PROP follow-ups, discovered via GitHub commit log that 3 new operator directives had been pushed to git in the past 24-48h but had NOT propagated to FUSE (workspace-sync direction is FUSE→git for operator-directives, no git→FUSE pull). Among them: DIRECTIVE-20260519-002 (HIGH priority, filed 2026-05-19T11:00Z) — decider win-new push dedup, source ISS-2134. Per dispatcher prompt: 'pending directives override Mode 1-4 selection logic; sort by priority descending, take top.' DIRECTIVE-20260519-002 is the highest pending priority and was authored against during this run.

findings.title: Operator-directives directory has FUSE-staleness: 3 git-only directives invisible to tinker's pre-flight FUSE scan.
findings.details: Pre-flight directive scan reads monitor/tinker/operator-directives/ from FUSE. Workspace-sync sync_glob for this directory is FUSE→git only (sync_glob writes to git but does not pull). build.js publish copies git→FUSE only for files classified 'git' in OWNERSHIP; operator-directives are classified 'append_only_glob', not 'git'. Result: directives written directly to git (via curl/PR/operator-direct commit) never reach FUSE. Three currently-pending directives are git-only: DIRECTIVE-20260518-002 (clone-depth, medium), DIRECTIVE-20260519-001 (third-inbox-writer, medium), DIRECTIVE-20260519-002 (decider win-new dedup, HIGH). This run discovered them via GitHub commit log inspection during PROP-046 follow-up verification, then fetched via curl. Next tinker run will face the same blind spot unless either (a) workspace-sync gains git→FUSE sync for operator-directives, OR (b) tinker pre-flight is extended to also check GitHub for newer directive files before falling through to FUSE-only scan.
findings.recommended_action: File PROP-049 (next-run candidate) proposing either (a) workspace-sync directional extension for append_only_glob directories, OR (b) tinker pre-flight git-side directive check. Operator preference between (a) and (b) is the open question — (a) is broader infrastructure, (b) is scoped to tinker only.
findings.evidence: Pre-flight returned 'NO PENDING DIRECTIVES'; GitHub API listing of monitor/tinker/operator-directives showed 3 new files (DIRECTIVE-20260518-002, DIRECTIVE-20260519-001, DIRECTIVE-20260519-002); FUSE ls confirmed absence.

findings.title: DIRECTIVE-20260519-002 marked completed via DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception.
findings.details: Fetched directive body from git via curl, added status='completed', completed_at='2026-05-19T12:12:00.000Z', completed_by_run='tinker-2026-05-19T12-05', prop_id_authored='PROP-048'. Wrote to FUSE. Universal-pusher workspace-sync will push the FUSE version to git on its next hourly cycle (~12:05 UTC tomorrow latest, likely much sooner). The FUSE write fully preserves the original directive body plus appends only the four allowed lifecycle fields per DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception.
findings.recommended_action: None — completed as part of this run.
findings.evidence: monitor/tinker/operator-directives/DIRECTIVE-20260519-002-decider-win-new-dedup.json now present in FUSE with status='completed'.

PROP-046.verification_method: Ran verification_pattern check_1 and check_2 (grep for 'recommended_lane' in both decider-intake.md and analyst-mode1-expansions.md). Both return 0 matches.

PROP-045.verification_method: Status field read from JSON. Operator-approved but no implementation amendment authored. PROP-045's targeted failure mode (stale-FUSE-overwrites-newer-git) is the exact pattern that almost bit this run too — the 4 git-only operator-directives discovered by GitHub commit-log inspection are a sibling instance: workspace cannot SEE files that git has but FUSE doesn't, and any in-flight FUSE edit on those files would create a divergence. PROP-045 implementation would mitigate the universal-pusher direction's contribution; the directive-discovery gap (FIND-2026-05-19T12-01) is the orthogonal git→FUSE pull half.

PROP-047.verification_method: Status field read from JSON. Earliest verification window ~2026-05-25 if operator applies in next 48h (curmudgeon needs 1-2 multi-cycle reviews after apply to verify the per-hole verification check landed in c-cycle reviews).
